rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for interface-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture during AGREE fusion by placing contracts in appropriate scopes and avoiding structural redesign.
Do not encode AADL properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
Avoid unsafe Boolean or numeric encodings of event ports; only use model-provided data values when available.
Do not invent new declarations in AGREE contracts; use only identifiers visible in the current component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings (e.g., assignments) must be placed in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees or bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL properties or event ports in unsafe numeric/Boolean contexts without model-provided data.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
